channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Russia launches a record number of drones into Ukraine on the eve of the war's 3-year anniversary
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched more strike drones into Ukraine overnight on Saturday than in any other single attack of the war, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Sunday, one day before the three-year anniversary of Moscow's full-scale invasion.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Musk gives all federal workers 48 hours to explain what they did last week or face consequences
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of federal workers have been given little more than 48 hours to explain what they accomplished over the last week, sparking confusion across key agencies as billionaire Elon Musk expands his drive to slash the size of federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: USPS honors beloved actress Betty White with commemorative stamp
item.description: The stamp features a digitally created portrait of White against a violet background with "bubbly spots that befit her sparkling personality.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: French aircraft carrier stages combat drills with Filipinos in disputed sea and visits Philippines
item.description: ABOARD THE CHARLES DE GAULLE, Philippines (AP) — France’s nuclear-powered aircraft carrier and accompanying warships were in the Philippines on Sunday after holding combat drills with Filipino forces in the disputed South China Sea in a show of firepower that would likely antagonize China.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ AND JOEAL CALUPITAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after Taliban lift suspension
item.description: An Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after the Taliban lifted their suspension over alleged cooperation with an overseas TV channel. Radio Begum launched on International Women’s Day in March 2021, five months before the Taliban seized power amid the chaotic withdrawal of U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kamala Harris receives prestigious Chairman's prize at NAACP Image Awards
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former Vice President Kamala Harris stepped on the NAACP Image Awards stage Saturday night with a sobering message, calling the civil rights organization a pillar of the Black community and urging people to stay resilient and hold onto their faith during the tenure of President Do
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Wounded, recovered and back to war. Ukrainian soldiers are returning to battle after amputation
item.description: DONETSK REGION, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian intelligence soldier doesn’t know how long his clinical death lasted after an explosive detonated beneath him. All Andrii Rubliuk remembers is overwhelming cold, darkness and fear.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, EVGENIY MALOLETKA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ohio bathroom law targeting transgender students has brought internal strife to some campuses
item.description: For some famously progressive colleges in Ohio, a new state law designed to keep transgender women from using women’s restrooms at schools is bringing a moment of soul-searching for students, alumni and administrators.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hawaii man freed after 30 years in prison visits mother's grave and ponders ubiquitous cellphones
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the first places Gordon Cordeiro visited when a judge ordered him released after spending 30 years in prison for a killing he always maintained he had nothing to do with was his mother’s Hawaii gravesite.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Border Patrol agent whose death is tied to cultlike Zizians is buried with military honors
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent whose killing in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border has been tied to a cultlike group was buried with full military honors Saturday at a national cemetery in Minnesota. David Maland, 44, died Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Pennsylvania Gov. Shapiro meets with family of fallen officer, victims of hospital shooting
item.description: "I am inspired by the amazing doctors, nurses, and staff at UPMC and WellSpan Health who did tremendous work to save lives in the face of tragedy," said Shapiro. "It was my honor to meet with them and to thank them for what they do in service to their neighbors.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, Fox 5

item.title: Oscar favorite ‘Anora’ wins best film, director and actor at the Independent Spirit Awards
item.description: Sean Baker's “Anora” won best film, best director and best actor for Mikey Madison at the Independent Spirit Awards on Saturday in what could be a preview of next Sunday’s Oscars: The film about a Brooklyn sex worker and her whirlwind affair with a Russian oligarch’s son has emerged in recent weeks
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Memorial grows for Pennsylvania officer killed in hospital shooting
item.description: A memorial is growing outside the police department in West York, a borough outside of York, Pennsylvania, after officer Andrew Duarte was killed in the line of duty on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NY governor makes case for Manhattan tolls in meeting with Trump after feds ordered a halt
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul made the case for Manhattan's congestion tolling during an Oval Office meeting with President Donald Trump after federal officials ordered a halt to the program, a spokesperson for the Democratic governor said.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thieves used a stolen card to buy a $523,000 lottery ticket. The victim wants to share the winnings
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Thieves used a stolen card to buy a winning French lottery ticket worth 500,000 euros ($523,000). But they vanished before cashing in —- and now they’re among France’s most famous fugitives. The man whose card was stolen, identified in police documents as Jean-David E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, ABC 4

item.title: Officer killed after a gunman took hostages at a Pennsylvania hospital
item.description: Authorities say a gunman entered a Pennsylvania hospital’s intensive care unit and took staff members hostage before he was killed by police in a shootout that also left an officer dead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: USDA scholarship for students at historically Black colleges suspended
item.description: A federal scholarship aimed at boosting students from underserved and rural areas attending historically Black colleges and universities has been put on hold.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: National Park Service rangers laid off as DOGE makes cuts
item.description: National Park Service ranger Mikayla Moors was one of thousands of government employees laid off by the DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, Fox 5

item.title: Universal reveals Shrek, SpongeBob lands for new theme park in Texas
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences recently revealed several themed areas that will be featured at Universal Kids Resort, a new family-focused theme park set to open next year in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KRON4

item.title: South Carolina death row inmate chooses firing squad as execution method
item.description: A South Carolina death row inmate has chosen to be executed by a firing squad next month rather than by lethal injection or the electric chair.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida woman who survived parents' murders as toddler reveals 'missing piece' after killer's execution
item.description: A woman who survived her parents' murders as a toddler reveals her "missing piece" after their killer's execution and her childhood memories connected to the horrific crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Killer nightclub hostess's deadly love affair with race car driver was 'eye for an eye': author
item.description: "A Cruel Love: The Ruth Ellis Story," a four-part drama series, was inspired by Carol Ann Lee's true-crime book, "A Fine Day for a Hanging: The Real Ruth Ellis Story.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has pleaded not guilty to multiple counts of murder, including murder as an act of terrorism, in the Dec. 4 killing of Brian Thompson outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, ABC 4

item.title: Judge upholds ban on DOGE accessing sensitive Treasury information, for now
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge in Manhattan on Friday extended a ban on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency accessing sensitive Treasury Department information, but rejected broader restrictions sought by 19 Democratic state attorneys general who sued over the issue. Judge Jeannette A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he may take control of the US Postal Service. Here's what to know
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said he may put the U.S. Postal Service under the control of the Commerce Department in what would be an executive branch takeover of the agency, which has operated as an independent entity since 1970.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Los Angeles DA Nathan Hochman does not support overturning Menendez brothers' murder convictions
item.description: Newly-elected Los Angeles District Attorney Nathan Hochman said at a Friday press conference that he has asked the court to reject the Menendez brothers' request for a new trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Diddy lawyer quits during federal sex crimes case, no reason given
item.description: Anthony Ricco notified the judge of his intent to withdraw after conversations with Diddy's lead counsel.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: A New Jersey man is convicted of attempted murder in the stabbing of Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — A New Jersey man was convicted Friday of attempted murder for stabbing author Salman Rushdie multiple times on a New York lecture stage in 2022.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Actress Hunter Schafer says she was issued a male passport
item.description: Hunter Schafer, one of Hollywood’s most visible transgender stars, said she was issued a U.S. passport with a male gender marker after her original passport, which listed her gender as female, was stolen.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: ICE, Homeland Security raid bakery, charge owners with harboring illegal immigrants
item.description: Bakery owners in Texas are facing felony charges after ICE and HSI arrested eight suspected illegal immigrants employed by the shop.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
